examples: I like my bacon extra crispy.
These fries are nice and crispy.
context: Hmm, curry, crispy roll. I feel like it might be one of those things that's salty and sweet, but I like very salty snacks. So I'm going to stick with pringles, but I've never tried this flavor before.

examples: Focus on pronunciation, not just grammar.
context: I will explain what I'm doing along the way and teach you English expressions and vocabulary that you can use in your daily life. I recommend shadowing me to practice your speaking and improve your pronunciation. First things first, let's pack a beach bag. I've got my beach day essentials in front of me. Obviously I need a swimsuit. Sometimes I wear a bikini, but today I'm packing my one piece. I don't want to get a sunburn, so I've got sunscreen and a sun stick for my face. I'm packing two beach towels and a book, so I don't get bored when I'm tanning.

examples: I was sweaty after the workout.
His sweaty hands made it hard to grip the rope.
It's normal to feel sweaty in a sauna.
context: It was nice being a beach bum, but I was getting hot and sweaty, so I changed back into my clothes, and I could use some AC, so I'm going to find a nice cafe with a good view of the beach.

examples: This place has a great vibe.
I'm getting good vibes from this restaurant.
The vibe at the concert was electric.
